shortAnswer: The U.S. economy leads in per capita GDP ($76,398 vs $12,720) and technological innovation, while China's economy is larger by total GDP ($17.9 trillion vs $27.4 trillion adjusted for PPP) and has become the world's largest exporter. The U.S. maintains higher living standards and institutional strength, while China offers faster growth rates and manufacturing dominance.

Messi has won more individual awards (8 Ballon d'Ors vs Ronaldo's 5) and a FIFA World Cup, while Ronaldo holds superior athleticism metrics and has scored more international goals (128 vs 129 for Messi as of 2026). Their peak performances occurred in different eras, with Messi dominating the 2009-2015 period and Ronaldo excelling from 2013-2018.
